target:
WIN-053 detail_evidence in data/wins.json — PATCH the existing string by inserting one new paragraph (with leading '

' separator) immediately AFTER the first paragraph that ends with the literal text "not a data disagreement; it is the dome's toroidal topology contradicting its own B(r) equation." and immediately BEFORE the second paragraph that begins "A second self-contradiction concerns the equation's functional form." No other paragraph is altered. detail_verdict_text, detail_extra, detail_claim, tldr_evidence, and tldr_verdict are unchanged.
patch_instructions:
In data/wins.json, locate the WIN with id='053'. In its detail_evidence field, find the first paragraph that ends with the verbatim sentence "This is not a data disagreement; it is the dome's toroidal topology contradicting its own B(r) equation." Immediately after that sentence (i.e., before the next '

' that introduces the 'A second self-contradiction concerns the equation's functional form.' paragraph), insert exactly one '

' followed by the new paragraph supplied in replacement_html. Do not modify any other paragraph in detail_evidence. Do not modify detail_verdict_text, detail_claim, detail_extra, tldr_evidence, or tldr_verdict. The TLDRs do not invoke the channelized-return defense and require no change.
replacement_html: A charitable reading of the dome's architecture might try to rescue the equation: perhaps the sub-terrestrial 'Sump' is not a uniform return spread across the full disc but a narrow channel whose cross-section approximates the polar aperture itself — flux would be conserved within that constrained tube, and B(r) would describe only what surface observers see, not the sub-terranean flux density. Two observations close this defense without our having to assume any specific return geometry. First, the dome's own 61% → 20% RMS improvement is computed against surface-observatory data — explicitly the INTERMAGNET ground-station network and the CHAOS-7 satellite-and-observatory composite that anchor mainstream geomagnetic models. The 64,852 nT southern coefficient is therefore being proposed by the dome itself as a surface value; reinterpreting it post hoc as a sub-terranean tube measurement disconnects the equation from the very dataset the dome cites to claim its improvement, and the 20% RMS number evaporates with it. Second, even granting a narrow return channel for the sake of argument, the dome would still owe us (a) the channel cross-section as an explicit, derived dome parameter rather than a geometry conjured to absorb this critique, and (b) a physical mechanism by which a narrow sub-crustal flux of order 65,000 nT propagates upward through thousands of kilometres of rock to produce the ~55,000 nT actually measured at the geographic south pole. That mechanism — multi-layer crustal magnetic conduction with depth-dependent susceptibility — is exactly the territory standard geomagnetism handles with degree-13 spherical harmonics anchored in geodynamo physics, and is precisely the territory the dome was constructed to leave behind. Either the southern coefficient is a surface prediction (in which case the 1,600:1 flux contradiction stands) or the dome borrows globe-style crustal physics to bridge a sub-terranean prediction up to the surface (in which case the architecture is no longer an alternative to the geodynamo, only a relabelling of it).
holes_addressed: WIN-053.c4 H2 (moderate, advocate-mode 'sub-terrestrial return channel' defense unpreempted): c3 rated this defense at 3/5 with a specific preemption recommendation; c4 confirmed the recommendation was not applied. The new paragraph closes the defense by (i) pinning the 64,852 nT coefficient as a SURFACE value via the dome's own validation method and (ii) showing that even granting a narrow return channel forces the dome to import standard crustal-conduction physics, defeating the architectural premise.
new_evidence_added: INTERMAGNET network as the explicit surface-observatory dataset against which the dome's RMS improvement is computed (https://intermagnet.github.io/)
CHAOS-7 as the satellite-and-observatory composite that anchors the comparison (https://spacecenter.dk/files/magnetic-models/CHAOS-7/)

Logical disjunction: surface-prediction reading (1,600:1 contradiction stands) vs sub-terranean-with-crustal-bridge reading (architecture collapses into a relabelled geodynamo). No third reading rescues the 20% RMS number.

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome could claim that B(r) is a phenomenological surface-fit equation while the toroidal geometry is metaphysical, so flux conservation need not apply at the surface.
anticipated_objections.response: That move concedes the central WIN-053 claim. WIN-053's headline is that the toroidal architecture explains the observed dipole via aetheric flow conservation. If B(r) is decoupled from the geometry, the geometry is no longer doing predictive work — and the entire 61% → 20% RMS improvement is being attributed to a fit that has no derivation from dome parameters, putting WIN-053 squarely back in the 'observed values relabeled' bucket already documented in paragraph 3 of detail_evidence.

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome could argue the 1,600:1 ratio is hostile because the polar aperture is unspecified and the disc-area return assumption is uncharitable.
anticipated_objections.response: The detail_evidence already shows that even doubling the aperture to 1,000 km only reduces the discrepancy to ~415×, and quadrupling to 3,000 km (six times the original estimate, larger than any feature the dome describes for the Axis Mundi) still gives ~46×. The defense fails by an order of magnitude across every aperture the dome plausibly claims, before the channelized-return reinterpretation is even invoked.

anticipated_objections.objection: Surface observations could in principle be reconciled with a sub-terranean source via dome-specific aetheric mechanics rather than crustal conduction.
anticipated_objections.response: Possible in principle, but unspecified. The dome currently provides no equation, parameter, or mechanism that bridges sub-terranean flux to surface field. Adding one would require new physics not in the V51.1 model and would itself be a fit to the same observed pole strengths it is trying to explain — re-running the exact circularity already documented for the original B(r) coefficients.

kernel_of_truth: A defender raising this objection has correctly identified the weakest joint in our argument as written: the 1,600:1 ratio implicitly assumes a uniform full-disc return, which the dome never explicitly states. A charitable reader could imagine a narrower geometry. Acknowledging that and then closing the door — via the dome's own surface-validation choice and the physical-mechanism question — turns a one-sided objection into a forced disjunction that the dome cannot satisfy without sacrificing either the 20% RMS claim or the architecture's independence from globe-physics.
cross_references: WIN-053 paragraph 1 (1,600:1 ratio) — the new paragraph immediately follows and preempts the natural defense.
WIN-053 paragraph 3 (coefficient circularity) — the surface-validation point reinforces this by showing the SURFACE reading is forced by the dome's own validation.
Section 6.1 / WIN-053 TLDR cross-link to §6.1 — unchanged; the 'observed values relabeled' framing is consistent.
unaddressed_holes_note: EXP-194 is scoped narrowly to ISS-1225 / H2 only, per the open-issues entry and tracker. Other WIN-053.c4 holes (H1 detail_verdict_text monitor.py boilerplate, H3 IGRF-13 coefficient count phrasing, H4 Sub-H δ sourcing, H5 north-pole field strength range, H6 3,000 km aperture sensitivity row, H7 flux-direction one-liner) were not assigned to EXP-194 and are out of scope for this expansion. Note however that H1, H5, and H3 appear ALREADY APPLIED in the current wins.json text I read at expansion time: detail_verdict_text contains the corrected 'Note that WIN-053 has no monitor.py domain at all' sentence (H1 fix), detail_evidence already cites '~54,000–56,000 nT at the North Magnetic Pole and ~65,000–67,000 nT at the South Magnetic Pole' (H5 fix), and detail_evidence already says '195 Gauss coefficients (degree 13 spherical harmonic expansion)' (H3 fix). The decider may wish to mark ISS for those three (if any exist as separate issues) as resolved by prior decider patches and to triage H4/H6/H7 separately. The 3,000 km aperture sensitivity (H6) is partially included in the curmudgeon's quantitative_verification but never made it into prose — a one-clause addition is still warranted but is not part of this expansion.
notes_for_decider:
Targeted single-paragraph insertion only. The patch is a string operation: split detail_evidence on the unique sentence "contradicting its own B(r) equation." then join with that sentence + '

' + new paragraph + remainder. Verify after patch that the WIN-053 detail_evidence still parses cleanly in the dashboard / index renderer (the new paragraph contains two anchor tags with target='_blank' — matching existing convention in detail_evidence). No JSON schema fields other than detail_evidence are touched. TLDRs (tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict) are intentionally not amended; both already foreground the 1,600:1 flux-conservation argument and the channelized-return defense was never the TLDR-level claim.
